title: Tapinanthus sacleuxii
taxonomy: Tapinanthus sacleuxii is a species of mistletoe in the family Loranthaceae. It is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the vascular system of a host tree. The Loranthaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that contain around 70 genera and over 900 species. This family is commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. The classification of Tapinanthus sacleuxii reflects its unique parasitic relationship with host trees.

intro: Tapinanthus sacleuxii, also known as the mistletoe mistletoe, is a parasitic plant species native to the island of Réunion. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Tapinanthus sacleuxii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tapinanthus sacleuxii are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to relieve symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
